variants.title: Donaldson P551021 Fuel Spin-On
variants.description: Genuine Donaldson P551021 Fuel Spin-On is engineered to deliver reliable performance and durability across a wide range of applications. Designed with high-quality materials, it ensures consistent functionality while meeting demanding operational requirements. Whether used in industrial, automotive, or heavy-duty settings, this component offers dependable operation and longevity. Its versatile design makes it suitable for various uses, supporting system efficiency and overall performance.
specifications: Efficiency 1 The efficiency of the element is shown using the Micron (µ) and Beta (β) ratings. The micron rating indicates the size of the particle being filtered and the beta rating the efficiency
calculated as 1/β. E.g. a rating of β200 will be 99.5% efficient whereas β1000 would be 99.9%.: 5µ @ β100

variants.title: Mann Filter WDK 11 102/2 Fuel Filter | Replaces the Donaldson P551021
variants.description: MANN-FILTER Fuel filters for high pressures remove unwanted and damaging dirt particles and water from the fuel and thus prevent possible damage due to wear and corrosion. Highest separation efficiency and sealing ensure functional reliability and protection during the entire maintenance interval / Reinforced or special materials suitable for use at elevated pressures.
specifications: Efficiency 1 The efficiency of the element is shown using the Micron (µ) and Beta (β) ratings. The micron rating indicates the size of the particle being filtered and the beta rating the efficiency
calculated as 1/β. E.g. a rating of β200 will be 99.5% efficient whereas β1000 would be 99.9%.: 5µ @ β100

variants.title: Fleetguard FF5633 Fuel Filter, Spin-On | Replaces the Donaldson P551021
variants.description: Fleetguard® FF5633 spin-on fuel filter delivers superior performance using proven media technology, removing harmful contaminants. Fleetguard fuel filters ensure optimal fuel system protection to meet or exceed OEM specifications.
specifications: Efficiency 1 The efficiency of the element is shown using the Micron (µ) and Beta (β) ratings. The micron rating indicates the size of the particle being filtered and the beta rating the efficiency
calculated as 1/β. E.g. a rating of β200 will be 99.5% efficient whereas β1000 would be 99.9%.: 5µ @ β100
